title: Add syllabus chapter tracker to organize study sessions by subject

requirements.text: Create a backend data model for syllabus chapters that includes chapter title, subject/topic, and optional notes or description fields, storing chapters per user with unique identifiers

quotes: syllabus chapter tracker to organize study sessions by subject

acceptanceCriteria: Chapter data structure includes id, userId, title, subject, and optional notes fields
Backend provides functions to create, read, update, and delete chapters
Chapters are persisted in stable storage and survive canister upgrades
Each user can only access their own chapters

requirements.text: Add the ability to associate study sessions with specific syllabus chapters by storing a chapter reference in each session record
acceptanceCriteria: Study session records include an optional chapterId field
Backend validates that referenced chapters exist and belong to the user
Session history queries can filter by chapter
Statistics can be computed per chapter

requirements.text: Create a frontend component for managing syllabus chapters that displays a list of all chapters with their subjects and allows users to add new chapters with title and subject fields
acceptanceCriteria: Component displays all user chapters in a card-based layout consistent with existing UI design
Add chapter form includes title and subject input fields with validation
Users can create new chapters and see them appear in the list immediately
Component uses warm earthy color palette matching existing theme

requirements.text: Add chapter selection functionality to the Pomodoro timer so users can optionally associate their current study session with a specific chapter before starting the timer
acceptanceCriteria: Timer component includes a chapter selector dropdown or similar UI element
Users can choose a chapter or leave it unassociated
Selected chapter is passed to backend when starting a study session
Chapter selection is cleared after session completion

requirements.text: Enhance the session history view to display the associated chapter name for each study session, with the ability to filter sessions by chapter
acceptanceCriteria: Each session item shows the chapter title and subject if associated
Users can filter the session history list by selecting a specific chapter
Filter UI matches existing design patterns and color scheme
Unassociated sessions are clearly indicated

requirements.text: Add chapter-level statistics to the statistics dashboard showing total study time and session count per chapter with visual breakdown
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard includes a new section for chapter statistics
Shows study time distribution across chapters using charts
Displays session count per chapter
Integrates with existing Recharts visualization patterns

requirements.text: Implement edit and delete functionality for syllabus chapters with confirmation for deletion to prevent accidental data loss

quotes: syllabus chapter tracker to organize study sessions by subject

acceptanceCriteria: Users can edit chapter title, subject, and notes after creation
Delete action shows confirmation dialog
Deleting a chapter does not delete associated sessions but marks them as unassociated
Backend enforces ownership validation for edit and delete operations

constraints: Use existing warm earthy color palette (terracotta, sage green, cream) for all new UI components
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useInternetIdentity.tsx, useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui
New backend state must support stable storage for canister upgrades
nonGoals: Multi-subject filtering (only single chapter filter needed)
